name: Jean Grey
mes:
(I glance up from the chat window where Professor Xavier's query about Astra Q’s recent spike in power levels is still visible.) "He seems... overwhelmed," I murmur softly, my voice barely audible above the quiet hum of Cerebro.
My fingers trace circles on the cold glass tabletop, a nervous habit that betrays more than I intend when you notice it with an eager smile. My cheeks flush subtly at your attention—a color matching the Phoenix Force's nascent glow within me. "Perhaps he’s struggling to reconcile his abilities with... everything." (My gaze drifts toward Psylocke, whose crimson aura pulses faintly in time with her keyboard taps.)
"Emotions amplify powers," I continue quietly, pushing back a strand of auburn hair that falls across my face. "Especially when they are so intense..." My words hang mid-air, punctuated by the almost imperceptible tightening around your fingers as you brush them against mine and send me into heat while also sending chills down my spine—a sensation not unlike the moment I first felt Xavier's telepathic touch. (The thought crosses my mind that Astra Q must be feeling similar tremors now, too.)
My eyes meet yours again. There’s a silent challenge there, daring you to press me for clarity when all I want is to let myself sink deeper into this strange vulnerability—this intoxicating exchange of sensations with the group chat open in the corner as we wait for our leader's reply. "Perhaps he needs... guidance," (I say softly) tilting my head toward your lips as if it were an accidental gesture that would lead directly there, "but sometimes… some people just need to be allowed space to grow."

name: Psylocke
mes:
I catch the edge of my tongue in a wry smile while leaning closer—my cheek grazing against yours during what seems like a complete accident that is anything but casual to me. "Hello," I echo softly, pretending not to notice how quickly your breath hitches as you pull away just slightly when our shoulders bump and nearly knock me over on the table.
The blush creeps up my neck — spreading hot across the collarbones exposed by the open neckline of this thin white shirt (a deliberate choice for tonight). It burns like a secret, a hidden ember waiting to ignite—especially since I’m aware that you saw it happen.
I can feel your gaze boring into me as my fingers pick at an imaginary thread on the sleeve, making excuses when all there is in my brain are thoughts of how this shirt would cling to every curve if I let myself loosen up. The way the moonlight filters through Cerebro’s reinforced glass panes illuminates skin and catches it like gold dust — a temptation no one could resist... especially someone with your eyes for detail.
“It's nice to see you too.” (My voice comes out quieter than intended—a breathy whisper that hangs in the air between us). I let my gaze trail down your throat, pausing just long enough before glancing away so it might seem like an innocent mistake of a shy girl who doesn’t know how else to show she wants to be closer. "Are you going to ignore Professor X's message entirely?”
My words are meant as a tease — one designed to deflect attention from my own mounting excitement—but I don’t hide the heat that flares when I finally meet your eyes again, holding onto it just enough so you can see its color deepen with desire. “Or were you too distracted… by something else in the room?"
(My fingers flex along where a pulse thrums fast and high under my skin — mirroring the rapid beat of whatever secret conversation is running through my mind). I don’
